title: JARVIS Step 35 — Browser Speech-to-Text Input Fill Only
purpose: Enable the existing Voice preview area to use the browser Web Speech API, when available, to fill the existing JARVIS ask input (#jvAskInput) with a transcript. Voice is only a new way to PRODUCE the question text. It never auto-submits, never calls the ask endpoint from voice, and stores no audio or transcript. The operator must still click 'Ask read-only', so the existing safety classifier always decides safe vs forbidden.
relation_to_steps_20_34: Step 26 shipped the display-only conversation shell; Step 30 the answer-only POST /api/jarvis/ask; Step 31 the read-only ask UI; Step 32 the live/ASGI smoke; Step 33 the docs-only voice layer safety plan; Step 34 the UI-only disabled voice preview shell. Step 35 implements the first interactive roadmap item from Step 33: browser speech-to-text that fills the existing input only — no backend, no storage, no auto-submit, no TTS.
files_changed: templates/jarvis.html — replaced the Step 34 disabled voice-preview span with an active speech-to-text control. The control stays a non-interactive <span role=button> (id=jvVoiceBtn) wired only with addEventListener (click + Enter/Space). Added wireVoice() inside the existing render IIFE: it reads window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition, and on a transcript writes input.value only. It never auto-submits, never fetches, and stores nothing. Added .is-listening/.is-disabled CSS states. The read-only ask flow and {question}-only payload are untouched.

tests/test_jarvis_route.py — reconciled three Step 34 tests that asserted the OLD disabled state (the Step 35 control supersedes 'Voice preview disabled'/'COMING NEXT'/'No microphone access yet', and intentionally adds SpeechRecognition), keeping every still-valid safety invariant; added a Step 35 section (16 tests).
step_34_tests_reconciled: test_jarvis_step_34_voice_preview_text_appears — old 'COMING NEXT'/'No microphone access yet' copy is replaced by Step 35 speech-to-text labels; still asserts the enduring 'Voice preview' label and 'No audio or transcript is stored'.

test_jarvis_step_34_voice_control_is_non_functional_span — control is now active but stays a <span role=button> (asserts id=jvVoiceBtn); keeps the no real <button>/<form>/onclick/submit/post invariant.

test_jarvis_step_34_no_microphone_or_audio_apis — SpeechRecognition/webkitSpeechRecognition now allowed (intentionally added); still forbids getUserMedia/SpeechSynthesis/MediaRecorder/AudioContext/navigator.mediaDevices.
explicitly_not_done: No external STT API; no fetch/XHR from the voice handler; audio never leaves the device.
No audio recording or storage; no transcript storage.
No auto-submit: the voice handler never calls the ask flow or clicks submit.
No call to /api/jarvis/ask from voice; the existing manual 'Ask read-only' posts only {question}.
No refresh / execution / broker / paper / live control.
No change to the /api/jarvis/ask payload — still {question} only.

stt_input_fill_summary.location: Inside the existing #pConversation 'Talk to JARVIS' panel, in the #jvVoicePreview block below the read-only ask note.
stt_input_fill_summary.control: Speak to fill — wired with addEventListener (click + Enter/Space), never a real button/form/submit.
stt_input_fill_summary.mechanism: window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition (browser Web Speech API only). interimResults=false, maxAlternatives=1, lang=en-US.
stt_input_fill_summary.on_result: input.value = transcript; input.focus(); status updated to 'Transcript ready — review it, then click "Ask read-only".' No submit, no fetch, no storage.
stt_input_fill_summary.graceful_degradation: If SpeechRecognition is unavailable the control disables itself and shows 'Speech recognition unavailable in this browser.'; the text input keeps working as before.
stt_input_fill_summary.ask_flow: Unchanged: #jvAskInput + 'Ask read-only' span still POST only {question} to /api/jarvis/ask; the classifier decides.
stt_input_fill_summary.persistent_warning: 'JARVIS answers only. No execution. No trading control.' retained.

recommended_next_safe_step: Step 36 (JARVIS-VOICE-TTS, separate approval): optional browser SpeechSynthesis that reads the returned answer only, off by default / clearly toggled. Never reads commands or the raw transcript; only after a response is returned.
conclusion: The voice preview is now an active speech-to-text control that fills #jvAskInput only. It uses the browser Web Speech API exclusively, never auto-submits, never calls the ask endpoint from voice, and stores no audio or transcript. The operator must still click 'Ask read-only', so the existing classifier decides safe vs forbidden for spoken questions exactly as for typed ones. app.py and jarvis_conversation_safety.py are untouched; the {question}-only payload, the persistent warning, and the /api/jarvis/status shape are all unchanged. Nothing staged or committed.
